agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, payment, promotions, and post-sale refund lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, promotional discount exclusivity, order immutability after payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — spanning cart operations, inventory reservation, payment confirmation, promotional pricing, and post-delivery refunds.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holding items in a cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated by a customer to finalize a purchase. Triggers inventory reservation for all cart items."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory units tied to a specific checkout session. Prevents other customers from purchasing the same units during the reservation window."},{"term":"Reservation Window","definition":"The 15-minute period after checkout begins during which reserved inventory is held. Expiration without payment releases the reservation."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment is not completed within the 15-minute reservation window."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently applied promotional pricing rule on a product. A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount to an order. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing."},{"term":"Promotional Pricing","definition":"A price reduction applied to a product via an active promotion, set by the business rather than a customer-entered code."},{"term":"Payment Confirmation","definition":"The irreversible event marking an order as paid. After this point, the order becomes immutable."},{"term":"Order Immutability","definition":"The state of an order after payment confirmation — no modifications to line items, quantities, or pricing are permitted."},{"term":"Delivery Date","definition":"The date a customer receives their order. Serves as the start of the 30-day refund eligibility window."},{"term":"Refund Eligibility Window","definition":"The 30-calendar-day period after delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Zero-Inventory Product","definition":"A product whose available inventory count is zero. It may remain listed/visible but cannot be added to a checkout or purchased."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to a cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is reserved only when the checkout process begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved on cart-add, the system state would be corrupt — phantom holds would deplete purchasable stock indefinitely. The reservation-on-checkout boundary is a core state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a product with 1 unit in stock, when two customers each add that product to their carts, then both carts reflect the item as available and no inventory reservation exists for either."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy threshold. The mechanism of time-limited reservation is invariant, but the specific duration is a tunable policy a VP could adjust.","testable_assertion":"Given a checkout started at T, when no payment is received by T+15 minutes, then all inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and units are returned to available stock."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have a maximum of one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous active promotions on a single product creates ambiguous pricing — the system cannot determine the correct price. This is a data consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"Given Product X has an active Promotion A, when an attempt is made to activate Promotion B on Product X, then the operation is rejected (or Promotion A must be deactivated first)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing both mechanisms simultaneously produces a corrupted price calculation — the system cannot guarantee which discount was intended or how they stack. This is a pricing integrity invariant.","testable_assertion":"Given an order containing a product with active promotional pricing, when a customer applies a discount code, then the system rejects the combination and enforces only one mechanism."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification breaks financial integrity — the payment amount was authorized against a specific order state. Allowing mutation creates accounting inconsistencies and potential fraud vectors.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with confirmed payment, when any modification attempt is made (add/remove items, change quantities, alter pricing), then the modification is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling a product with no available inventory would create an unfulfillable order — a violation of physical constraint and state integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given a product with zero available inventory, when a customer attempts to begin checkout with that product, then the checkout is blocked for that product with an appropriate error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds must be requested within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy. The concept of a refund window is important, but the specific duration is a policy decision that could be changed (e.g., extended during holidays).","testable_assertion":"Given an order delivered on day D, when a refund is requested on day D+31, then the refund request is rejected as outside the eligibility window."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer has not initiated checkout","then":"No inventory reservation exists for those items, and other customers can still purchase the same items"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"The checkout process begins","then":"Inventory is reserved for all items in the cart, reducing available stock for other customers"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A checkout session has been initiated","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment completion","then":"All inventory reservations for that session are released and available stock is restored"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer completes payment within the 15-minute window","when":"Payment is confirmed","then":"The inventory reservation is converted to a permanent allocation, the order is finalized, and the order becomes immutable"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"Product X already has an active Promotion A","when":"An administrator attempts to activate Promotion B on Product X","then":"The system rejects the activation or requires deactivation of Promotion A first"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order contains items with active promotional pricing","when":"The customer attempts to apply a discount code","then":"The system rejects the discount code application and informs the customer that promotional pricing and discount codes cannot be combined"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order contains items without promotional pricing","when":"The customer applies a valid discount code","then":"The discount code is applied to the order"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order has confirmed payment","when":"Any attempt is made to modify the order (items, quantities, addresses, pricing)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product has zero available inventory and is still listed in the catalog","when":"A customer attempts to begin checkout with this product","then":"The checkout is blocked for this product with a clear error message, while the product remains visible in the catalog"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected as outside the eligibility window"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A customer has started checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"Another customer attempts to purchase the same last-unit item","then":"The second customer is informed the item is unavailable due to insufficient stock"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A product in the customer's cart has its last unit reserved by another checkout","when":"The first customer initiates their own checkout","then":"The checkout fails for that product with an out-of-stock error"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer starts checkout, reservation is created, then inventory is reduced to zero by admin while reservation is active","expected_behavior":"The existing reservation should remain valid — the reservation was created when stock was available. The admin reduction applies to unreserved stock only."},{"scenario":"A discount code is applied to an order, then a product in the order subsequently has a promotion activated before payment","expected_behavior":"The discount code was applied first; the system should either honor the discount code (since it was applied to a non-promotional product at application time) or re-validate and force the customer to choose one mechanism."},{"scenario":"Payment confirmation arrives at exactly the 15-minute boundary","expected_behavior":"The system must use a consistent rule (e.g., reservation expires strictly AFTER 15 minutes, or at-or-after). A race condition here must be resolved deterministically — the payment should either succeed or fail, never partially."},{"scenario":"Customer adds a zero-inventory product to cart (cart doesn't reserve), then starts checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation must validate current inventory. If the product has zero inventory at checkout time, checkout is blocked for that item."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on exact day 30 after delivery","expected_behavior":"Day 30 is within the 30-day window and should be accepted. The boundary should be clearly defined (inclusive of day 30)."},{"scenario":"A promotion is deactivated mid-checkout after the promotional price was displayed to the customer","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for the duration of the reservation window. Changing the price mid-checkout creates trust and legal issues."},{"scenario":"Multiple discount codes applied in rapid succession","expected_behavior":"Only one discount code can be applied per order. The system should accept the first valid code and reject subsequent attempts, or replace the existing code with the new one — but never stack them."},{"scenario":"Product inventory drops to zero between cart-add and checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Since cart does not reserve inventory, the checkout initiation must fail for the out-of-stock product with an appropriate message."},{"scenario":"Abandoned checkout releases reservation, but customer returns and tries to check out again","expected_behavior":"A new checkout session is created with a fresh reservation attempt. If inventory is still available, the reservation succeeds. If another customer purchased during the gap, the checkout fails."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation could lead to overselling (unfulfillable orders, compensation costs) or underselling (lost revenue from phantom holds). Discount stacking bugs could erode margins. Refund window enforcement protects against late return abuse.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation timeout directly impacts checkout conversion rates and inventory throughput. Promotion exclusivity simplifies pricing calculation and reduces support escalations from confused customers. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline — no mid-flight changes.","user":"Customers expect that items visible in the catalog are purchasable — zero-inventory blocking at checkout (not at listing) may cause frustration. Clear communication about discount exclusivity prevents checkout surprise. The 15-minute window must be clearly communicated to avoid perceived lost carts.","risk":"Race conditions at the reservation boundary and payment timeout boundary are the highest-risk technical scenarios. Discount code + promotion mutual exclusivity must be enforced at the domain level, not just the UI, to prevent API-level abuse. Order immutability must be enforced at the persistence layer, not just the application layer.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly experience inventory availability, discount application rules, checkout time pressure, and refund window enforcement."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage single-promotion-per-product constraint. Cannot run stacked promotions — requires workflow adjustment."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability and single-discount-mechanism rules simplify revenue recognition and reconciliation. Refund window provides predictable liability exposure."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle edge cases around expired reservations, discount exclusivity complaints, and refund window boundary disputes."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Depends on inventory reservation accuracy — overselling creates pick-and-pack failures and customer escalations."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must implement deterministic race condition handling at reservation and payment boundaries. Must enforce invariants at the domain layer, not just UI."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear and internally consistent with no contradictions. Rules are explicit and unambiguous for the core flows. Confidence is not higher because several edge cases are unaddressed in the spec: (1) which discount mechanism takes priority when both could apply, (2) whether the refund window is calendar or business days, (3) whether the 15-minute timeout is configurable, (4) price-lock behavior during checkout, and (5) whether order immutability extends to shipping address. These are reasonable gaps for a product team spec but create ambiguity for implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is reserved at checkout, not at cart-add","15-minute reservation timeout releases inventory","One active promotion per product","Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive","Orders are immutable after payment confirmation","Zero-inventory products cannot be purchased","Refund window exists tied to delivery date"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which discount mechanism wins when both are available — does promotional pricing take precedence, or does the customer choose?","Whether the 30-day refund window uses calendar days or business days","Whether order immutability applies to ALL fields (shipping address, gift message) or only financial fields (items, quantities, pricing)","Price-lock behavior during the 15-minute checkout window if promotions change","Whether the zero-inventory check happens at cart-add, checkout-initiation, or payment-time (spec says checkout, but cart-add UX guidance is missing)"],"assumptions":["The 30-day refund window uses calendar days inclusive of the delivery date","Order immutability applies to all order attributes, not just line items","The 15-minute timeout is measured from checkout initiation, not from the last customer interaction","Inventory is checked at checkout initiation for the reservation, and insufficient stock blocks checkout for the affected items","When both a discount code and promotional pricing could apply, the system rejects the combination rather than silently choosing one"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When a product has active promotional pricing and a customer applies a discount code, the spec says they cannot combine — but does not specify which takes priority. Does the promotional price automatically apply and the discount code is rejected? Or does the customer choose which discount mechanism to use?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Merchandising Team","Engineering"],"options":["Promotional pricing always takes priority; discount codes are rejected for promotional products","Customer chooses which discount mechanism to apply","Discount code takes priority if entered; promotional pricing is the default fallback"],"recommendation":"Promotional pricing should auto-apply as the default (it's business-driven), and discount codes should be rejected for items already under promotion. This is simplest to implement and prevents customer gaming."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states orders cannot be modified after payment, but does not clarify scope. Does immutability cover only financial attributes (line items, quantities, pricing) or all attributes including shipping address, gift messages, and delivery instructions?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support","Fulfillment"],"options":["Full immutability — no changes to any order field after payment","Financial immutability only — shipping address and non-financial metadata can be updated until fulfillment begins","Immutability with a separate 'amendment' process that creates a linked correction record"],"recommendation":"Financial immutability (items, quantities, pricing) should be absolute. Shipping address changes should be allowed until the order enters fulfillment, as this is standard e-commerce practice and reduces support burden."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, payment, promotions, and post-sale refund lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, promotional discount exclusivity, order immutability after payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order Management & Checkout — spanning cart operations, inventory reservation, payment confirmation, promotional pricing, and post-delivery refunds.","ubiquitous_lan
